_meta.draft_status: initial draft by Claude Code, Thu Apr 23 2026 — REQUIRES Barbara's curation. Spec §8 is explicit: the library is curated by Barbara, not generated by an agent. The agent's job is selection and sequencing, not discovery.
notes_for_barbara: I have NOT generated specific URLs. Khan Academy video IDs, Illustrative Mathematics task numbers, and YouTube URLs all rot and I can't verify them. What I've provided is: resource CATEGORIES with known source sites, modalities, descriptions of what they teach, and which misconceptions they target. You fill in the specific URLs during curation.
Target count is 15-25 per spec §8. I've drafted 20 skeleton entries: 2-3 per misconception, spanning modalities (video / manipulative / worksheet / game).
Per spec: 'fewer, better, more actionable' — prefer quality over count.
Montessori materials are physical — URLs don't apply; the description points to the material name and where to get it.
The `source_site` field holds the top-level site (e.g., khanacademy.org) so you can quickly verify each curation decision is in a trusted source.
When you curate: delete resources you don't like, add ones you do, replace descriptions that don't match how your setting uses them.

resources.title: [TO CURATE] Introduction to equivalent fractions — video
resources.url: [TO VERIFY — Khan Academy has an 'Equivalent fractions intro' video under the 3rd grade fractions unit]
resources.notes: Khan's equivalent-fractions intro uses a visual area model, which addresses the 'they look different' intuition directly.

resources.title: Fraction tiles / fraction bars — hands-on manipulative
resources.source_site: physical material — Lakeshore, Didax, or equivalent
resources.notes: Physical fraction bars (halves, thirds, fourths, sixths, eighths, twelfths all the same overall length). The learner can lay 2/4 on top of 1/2 and see they match. Excellent for m01 because it makes piece-size explicit: 1/8 piece is visibly smaller than 1/4 piece.

resources.title: [TO CURATE] Illustrative Mathematics — fraction task (3rd grade)
resources.url: [TO VERIFY — Illustrative Mathematics has open-access tasks tagged by CCSS standard]
resources.notes: Look for tasks under CCSS 3.NF.A.1 (understanding fractions as equal parts) — often involves partitioning shapes.

resources.title: [TO CURATE] Math Antics — Fractions are Parts — video
resources.notes: Math Antics tends to be the video style that engages kids who dislike Khan. Use as alternative when a kid rejects Khan for tone reasons.

resources.title: Paper folding for equivalent fractions — hands-on activity
resources.source_site: teacher-led activity — no URL, description follows
resources.notes: Fold a strip of paper in half, mark 1/2. Unfold. Fold again (now into fourths); see that the original 1/2 mark now aligns with 2/4. Repeat: fold again for eighths; same crease is at 4/8. Directly shows that the multiplicative doubling of denominator pairs with doubling of numerator — confronts the additive-equivalence misconception visually.

resources.title: [TO CURATE] Number line fractions — interactive applet
resources.source_site: mathlearningcenter.org (Number Pieces / Number Line apps) OR geogebra.org
resources.notes: A number-line app lets the learner place fractions on a continuum from 0 to 2+, which directly addresses m03 (fractions > 1 exist) and supports comparison work.

resources.title: [TO CURATE] Khan Academy — Comparing fractions with different denominators
resources.notes: Khan's comparison video explicitly addresses the 'bigger denominator' misconception using area-model visuals.

resources.title: Two-pizza comparison — real-world discussion activity
resources.notes: Cut two paper 'pizzas' of visibly different sizes. Each into fourths. Ask: 'Is 1/4 of this pizza the same as 1/4 of that pizza?' Make the size difference obvious. The referent-whole insight lands when the kid says the words 'it depends on the pizza size' in their own voice.

resources.title: [TO CURATE] Open Up Resources — Fraction lessons
resources.url: [TO VERIFY — Open Up has a 4th grade fractions unit]
resources.notes: Full lessons with problems sequenced from like to unlike denominators. Good for consolidation work.

resources.title: [TO CURATE] Khan Academy — Adding fractions with like denominators
resources.notes: Direct instruction on why you add numerators but not denominators.

resources.title: Montessori fraction circles / fraction insets — physical manipulative
resources.source_site: Montessori material — Nienhuis, Alison's Montessori, Ikon, or similar Montessori supplier
resources.notes: Classic Montessori circular fraction insets: whole, halves, thirds, fourths, fifths, sixths, eighths, ninths, tenths. Kids can physically swap 2/4 pieces for 1/2 piece and confirm equivalence by matching perimeter. Strong fit for mastery-based settings.

resources.title: [TO CURATE] Fractions on a number line — Khan Academy
resources.notes: Number line representation grounds fractions as single quantities.

resources.title: Chef's measuring cup challenge — kitchen activity
resources.notes: Measuring cups come in 1, 1/2, 1/3, 1/4. Give a recipe asking for 3/4 cup with only a 1/4 cup. Kid discovers 3 × 1/4 = 3/4. Scale up to '2/3 cup using 1/6 scoop' to build fluency. Applied mastery is the key test per spec §1.

resources.title: [TO CURATE] Improper fractions and mixed numbers — Khan Academy
resources.notes: Addresses m03 with explicit examples of fractions > 1.

resources.title: [TO CURATE] GeoGebra — Fraction comparison sandbox
resources.url: [TO VERIFY — GeoGebra has multiple user-built fraction comparison applets]
resources.notes: Interactive sliders let the learner drag denominator values and watch piece size change. Good for building the 'more pieces = smaller pieces' intuition.

resources.title: Fraction war — card game
resources.source_site: printable fraction cards — many open sources
resources.notes: Like the card game 'war' but with fraction cards. Each player flips a card; bigger fraction takes both. Builds comparison fluency with repeated reps. Good for small-group practice.

resources.title: [TO CURATE] Adding fractions with unlike denominators — Khan Academy
resources.notes: Introduces common denominators using area models.

resources.title: Cuisenaire rods — fraction sense builder
resources.source_site: physical material — Didax, ETA, or similar
resources.notes: Cuisenaire rods are the classic tool for referent-whole reasoning: if orange (10 cm) is 'the whole', what is white (1 cm)? 1/10. But if red (2 cm) is 'the whole', what is white? 1/2. Directly confronts m06 (the whole doesn't matter).

resources.title: [TO CURATE] Prodigy Math or similar adaptive game — fraction level
resources.url: [TO VERIFY — Prodigy has fraction content; check existing game selection first]
resources.notes: Included cautiously — games are motivating but often don't target specific misconceptions well. Use for practice fluency, not diagnosis.

resources.title: Draw-your-own fraction picture — explain-your-thinking task
resources.notes: Give the kid a blank sheet. Prompt: 'Draw a picture that shows what 2/3 means. Then draw a picture that shows why 2/3 and 4/6 are the same.' The act of drawing surfaces misconceptions that typed-answer problems can hide. Useful as a guide-administered probe between assessments.
